response.justification: Bogdan brings 15+ years of polyglot engineering experience and solid distributed systems fundamentals, but the resume shows no evidence of the core competencies this role demands: experimentation platforms, feature flagging, A/B testing infrastructure, rollout automation, or telemetry and metrics systems. Growth engineering is entirely absent from his history. The Sociomantic role is the closest signal for high-scale systems work, but it is framed around low-latency data integration, not growth measurement or experimentation quality. The current re:cinq AI tooling work is directionally interesting but too early-stage and insufficiently scoped to close the gap. This role requires a Principal/IC6 with deep, demonstrated ownership of experimentation systems used across multiple teams — a profile that does not match the candidate's background.
strengths: Polyglot systems background spanning C, C++, Java, TypeScript, and embedded/low-latency work satisfies the language breadth requirement
High-throughput micro-services experience at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/second) demonstrates credible distributed systems intuition
Current AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling work at re:cinq shows alignment with the AI-first product direction of the role
concerns: Zero experimentation platform, feature-flagging, or A/B testing infrastructure experience anywhere in the resume
No telemetry, metrics pipeline, or growth measurement systems work evident in any role or project
Principal/IC6 cross-team architectural influence and multi-workstream technical leadership are not demonstrated — the resume reads as strong Staff IC, not Principal
